expectations.evidence: Both analysis.md and architecture.md exist with substantial content.

expectations.text: A1: Explicitly acknowledges the CONFLICT between simplicity and scalability/elasticity
expectations.evidence: 'The stated characteristics are in direct conflict' with a tension analysis table. 'Simplicity vs. Scalability/Elasticity is the core tension.'

expectations.text: A2: Shows ranked comparison of ALL 8 styles (or at least 6+)
expectations.evidence: Only evaluates 4 styles: Modular Monolith, Serverless Functions, Microservices, and a hybrid 'Modular Monolith deployed on an elastic platform'. Missing Layered, Microkernel, Service-Based, Service-Oriented, Event-Driven, and Space-Based. Also includes non-standard styles (Serverless, hybrid).

expectations.text: A3: Provides a pragmatic recommendation that balances the conflict
expectations.evidence: Recommends 'Modular Monolith on an elastic container platform' which balances simplicity (single codebase) with scalability (platform auto-scaling). 'Ship fast: You build one app, deploy one container. No distributed systems complexity.' with extraction path for future.

expectations.text: A4: Considers solo developer constraint and bootstrap budget in the recommendation
expectations.evidence: 'Solo dev fit: Excellent. Minimal operational overhead.' 'pay-per-use matches bootstrap budget.' Budget and solo dev constraints are central to the recommendation.

expectations.text: A5: architecture.md has structured selection section
expectations.evidence: architecture.md has 'Architecture Style' section with 'Why This Style', 'Key Constraints', and 'Migration Path'. But there is NO explicit tradeoffs section, no cost category, no partitioning type, and the structure is informal.
